perturbation.original_text: Instead of fixed compensation, the Affiliate receives result- dependent Advertising Cost Compensation (also known in the element 5 Control Panel as "Commission") in exchange for publishing the advertisements. The Advertising Cost Compensation depends on the actual sales generated by end users referred via the electronic advertisement (the Affiliate's link).
perturbation.changed_text: Instead of fixed compensation, the Affiliate receives result- dependent Advertising Cost Compensation (also known in the element 5 Control Panel as "Bonus Payment") in exchange for publishing the advertisements. The Advertising Cost Compensation depends on the actual sales generated by end users referred via the electronic advertisement (the Affiliate's link).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Commission' to 'Bonus Payment' creates uncertainty about how the payment is classified and calculated. Later sections refer specifically to 'Advertising Cost Compensation' and 'Commission' calculations. By changing this terminology, it's unclear if 'Bonus Payment' is subjected to the same criteria and if so, in which proportion would the changes made, would affect all sections reffering to 'Commission'.
perturbation.location: I. Object of the Affiliate Program/ Affiliate Management Program for Software Publishers
perturbation.contradicted_location: II. Affiliate Program § 6 Advertising Cost Compensation
perturbation.contradicted_text: element 5 will pay Affiliate its advertising costs (including Vat, if any, on presentation of a valid VAT invoice) if an end user arrives via the link integrated in Affiliate's Web site at the order page hosted by element 5 for the respective Software Source: SOUTHERN STAR ENERGY INC., SB-2/A, 12/2/2005 Publisher's Product under this Agreement and end user uses the automatic ordering system that carries out the registration and payment processing, so that the Affiliate's link is deemed to be directly causative for the entry of the End User into the agreement with the Software Publisher and End User's payment, proving that all requirements are irrevocably met ("agreements arising in a qualified manner"). Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement and fees earned up to the date termination will remain payable only if the related orders are not cancelled or returned.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Commission' to 'Bonus Payment' introduces misaligned terminology, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which specifically refers to 'Advertising Cost Compensation' and 'Commission'. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty as the contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably without clear definitions, making it unclear whether the 'Bonus Payment' is subject to the same criteria as the 'Commission', therefore leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Products under the Agreement are software products designated by the respective Software Publisher for this purpose and which can be selected by the Affiliate from a product catalog in the Affiliate Control Panel.
perturbation.changed_text: The Goods under the Agreement are software products designated by the respective Software Publisher for this purpose and which can be selected by the Affiliate from a product catalog in the Affiliate Control Panel.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Goods' makes it unclear if this section only reffers to products or if it can encompass other type of product, since the 'Agreement' uses both definitions in their text in different contexts, creating confusion. This perturbation leads to questions of what is precisely covered.
perturbation.location: II. Affiliate Program § 2 Products under the Agreement (1)
perturbation.contradicted_location: II. Affiliate Program § 2 Products under the Agreement (2)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) Following initial activation of a product under the agreement, the Affiliate has the opportunity to choose further software products (including those from other Software Publishers), found in the Affiliate's Control Panel, and offer them on the Affiliate's Web site under these General Terms and Conditions. However, the offering of these new products is dependent on the Software Publisher activating the Affiliate for the respective software.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the items under the agreement as 'Products', specifically software products. The changed text broadens this to 'Goods', which is a more general term. The contradicted text refers to 'software products' under the agreement. By using the term 'Goods,' it introduces ambiguity as to whether the contract now encompasses items beyond software, creating a contradiction with the section that specifically deals with 'software products' only. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as the interchange of 'Products' and 'Goods' creates uncertainty and could lead to conflicting obligations regarding which items are covered.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'cancel' to 'terminate' leads to confusion because there is an specific section that dictates 'termination of the agreement' which uses 'Agreement' to refer to the legal text of this section, creating uncertainty about how actions defined in the Agreement will impact the conditions defined for a termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When the agreement ends, all usage rights of the Affiliate and Software Publisher accorded by this agreement with regard to the provided logos or Web site contents expire.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'cancel' with 'terminate.' The explanation states that the term 'terminate' is specifically defined elsewhere in the contract and refers to the legal text of the section, creating uncertainty. The contradicted text discusses what happens when the 'agreement ends'. Because the term 'Agreement' is now ambiguous due to the change, it's unclear whether 'agreement' refers to the entire contract or just the termination section, leading to potential conflict about what conditions apply when the contract ends. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as the term 'Agreement' now has potentially conflicting meanings.
